note: v2 contract. The root token-contract.json remains the legacy/published brand-config contract until the consumer swap.

accordion.trigger.focusRingWidth.description: focus-visible:ring-2 with a 2px offset — not the 3px form-control ring.

alert.action.reservedWidth.description: Right padding reserved for the action slot (pr-18 = 72px — no space token at 18).

alert.gap.description: Row gap between title and description.

alert.iconGap.description: Column gap when the alert has a leading icon.

alertDialog.content.maxWidthDesktop.description: Size default widens to max-w-sm (384px) from the sm breakpoint; size sm stays at 320.

alertDialog.description.minHeight.description: Two lines of Body/sm (2 × the 20px line box) are RESERVED, even when the copy is one line. Without it the dialog changes height between tones and between locales — Thai runs longer than English — so the buttons jump under the cursor the moment the copy changes. Reserve the space; do not let the layout breathe.

alertDialog.footer.gap.description: Between the footer buttons. REMOVED 2026-07-13: the footer had a bg-muted/50 band with a top rule and a bleed past the content padding. That band's only job is to separate actions from CONTENT THAT SCROLLS — most dialogs are short, so it was drawing a divider that divided nothing and made one box read as two stacked cards. shadcn's own footer carries no surface; this kit had added it. The footer is now just the actions.

alertDialog.media.size.description: Media box, not a touch target — pinned to 40px when size.control.xl moved to 44.

avatar.group.overlap.description: Applied as a NEGATIVE margin (-space-x-2) so avatars overlap by 8px.

avatar.ringWidth.description: after: pseudo-ring on the avatar itself.

avatar.size.lg.description: Avatar lg is a box, not a touch target — pinned to 40px when size.control.xl moved to 44.

blockquote.blockGap.description: Between stacked children inside the quote.

breadcrumb.gap.description: Gap between crumbs in the list.

breadcrumb.pageFontWeight.description: The current page is not bolded — it is distinguished by color only.

button.fontSize.sm.description: Literal in source (text-[0.8rem]) — between xs and sm scale steps.

button.minWidth.md.description: A one-word button ("OK", "ตกลง") is a tiny, hard-to-hit target and reads as an afterthought. The floor is 64px — roughly two Thai words at this size — so buttons in a row also line up instead of jittering to their label width. NOTE: the icon-* sizes do NOT get this; an icon button is a square.

button.radius.description: rounded-lg = var(--radius). xs/sm sizes clamp via rounded-[min(var(--radius-md),10|12px)].

buttonGroup.gap.description: Only between NESTED groups — buttons inside one group sit flush.

buttonGroup.radius.description: The group's outer corners; inner ones collapse to 0.

buttonGroup.separator.inset.description: mx-px / my-px — a hairline inset so the rule does not touch the outer border.

calendar.cellRadius.description: [--cell-radius:var(--radius-md)] = 8px. Range ends round only on their outer side.

calendar.cellSize.description: 32px. Upstream ships 28 (--spacing(7)). 32 buys a real hit target on a day cell and gives the caption 248px to work in — the caption needs 217 (month select 85 + year select 64 + two 28px nav buttons + gaps), so it fits with slack instead of on the edge. 36 was overkill: it solved a problem caused by hard-coded select widths, not by the cell.

calendar.day.gap.description: Between the date and its below-label (e.g. a dot or price).

calendar.day.rangeBleed.description: after:w-4 — the muted band the range ends paint past the cell so the row reads continuous.

calendar.monthGap.description: Between months (and between caption and grid).

calendar.nav.buttonSize.description: 28px — deliberately SMALLER than a day cell: paging is a secondary move next to picking, and the selects are the fast path.

calendar.padding.description: Transparent inside a card or popover.

calendar.weekday.fontSize.description: 12px. FIX 2026-07-14: was text-[0.8rem] (12.8px) — the same off-scale literal Toggle carried. It lands on a half pixel and exists on no type scale.

carousel.nav.offset.description: Applied as a NEGATIVE inset (-left-12 / -right-12 / -top-12) — the buttons sit 48px outside the viewport edge.

chart.heatmap.cellSize.description: 12px. Small enough that a year fits on one row of a dashboard, big enough to hover. Below ~10px the cell stops being a target and becomes a texture.

chart.heatmap.gap.description: 4px. The gap is what makes the grid readable as DAYS — remove it and the year turns into a heat smear with no unit.

chart.heatmap.radius.description: A soft square. A circle would read as a dot plot; a hard square reads as a pixel.

chart.indicator.radius.description: rounded-[2px] — literal in source, matches radius.xs but is written as an arbitrary value.

chart.tooltip.borderWidth.description: border-border/50 — the border color runs at 50%.

chart.tooltip.shadow.description: shadow-xl — Tailwind's default; deeper than shadow.lg, the largest step our scale carries.

chart.tooltip.valueFontFamily.description: Values are mono + tabular-nums so digits do not jitter on hover.

chat.bubble.groupedRadius.description: The corners facing the neighbouring bubble in a group (rounded-tl-md / -bl-md, mirrored for the user side).

chat.bubble.maxWidth.description: max-w-prose — a ch-based measure, not a px token.

chat.composer.focusRingWidth.description: focus-within:ring-1 — a hairline ring, not the 3px form ring.

chat.message.gap.description: Between the bubble and its name/meta lines.

citation.marker.fontSize.description: text-[0.625rem] = 10px — below font.size.xs (12px), literal in source.

color.accent.description: Interactive hover, focus, and active surfaces.

color.accent-foreground.description: Interactive hover, focus, and active surfaces.

color.background.description: The default app background and text color.

color.background-color.description: Kit extra — not part of the shadcn contract. Prefer a shadcn-named role.

color.brand-accent.description: Kit extra — not part of the shadcn contract. Prefer a shadcn-named role.

color.brand-accent-foreground.description: Kit extra — not part of the shadcn contract. Prefer a shadcn-named role.

color.card.description: Elevated surfaces and the content inside them.

color.card-foreground.description: Elevated surfaces and the content inside them.

color.destructive-border.description: Destructive border on a destructive surface. Dark: red.800.

color.foreground.description: The default app background and text color.

color.input.description: Form control borders and input surface treatment.

color.popover.description: Floating surfaces and the content inside them.

color.popover-foreground.description: Floating surfaces and the content inside them.

color.primary-hover.description: Kit extra — not part of the shadcn contract. Prefer a shadcn-named role.

color.primary-surface.description: Primary surface (solid tint): selected row, file-upload dropzone-active. Dark: brand-primary.950.

color.secondary.description: Lower-emphasis filled actions and supporting surfaces.

color.secondary-foreground.description: Lower-emphasis filled actions and supporting surfaces.

color.secondary-hover.description: Secondary control hover fill (solid). Dark: gray.700.

color.secondary-surface-hover.description: Secondary badge hover surface (solid). Dark: gray.800.

color.semantic-background.description: Kit extra — not part of the shadcn contract. Prefer a shadcn-named role.

color.semantic-border.description: Kit extra — not part of the shadcn contract. Prefer a shadcn-named role.

color.semantic-foreground.description: Kit extra — not part of the shadcn contract. Prefer a shadcn-named role.

color.sidebar.description: The base sidebar surface and default sidebar text.

color.sidebar-accent.description: Hover and selected states inside the sidebar.

color.sidebar-accent-foreground.description: Hover and selected states inside the sidebar.

color.sidebar-foreground.description: The base sidebar surface and default sidebar text.

combobox.chip.height.description: h-[calc(--spacing(5.5))] = 22px — literal in source, no space token at 5.5.

combobox.chip.removeOpacity.description: The X on a chip is half-strength until hovered: it must be reachable, but it should not turn every chip into a row of delete buttons.

combobox.chips.gap.description: Between chips, and between the last chip and the typing area.

combobox.chips.minHeight.description: The chips box IS the trigger in a multi-select (min-h-9). A min-height, not a height: the box grows as chips wrap onto new lines.

combobox.chipsInput.minWidth.description: The typing area that trails the chips. It keeps a 64px floor so there is always somewhere to type — without it, enough chips would squeeze the caret out of the box entirely.

combobox.item.paddingRight.description: Room for the trailing check indicator.

combobox.item.radius.description: CONCENTRIC: --combobox-content-radius − --combobox-list-padding = 4px. A nested corner must be the outer radius MINUS the padding, or the gap at the corner is not the gap along the edge — the inner box looks either pinched or rattling around inside its container. Was picked off the radius scale by eye; now it is derived.

combobox.trigger.chipPaddingX.description: Trigger padding when it holds chips (single-cap key: the kebab pass only splits at one capital, so paddingXChips would emit --…-padding-xchips).

combobox.trigger.iconSize.description: Grows to size-5 on coarse pointers.

combobox.trigger.paddingX.description: Tightens to px-1.5 when the trigger holds chips.

command.empty.paddingY.description: The 'no results' block. Generous vertical space on purpose: an empty list should read as an answer, not as a panel that failed to load.

command.input.radius.description: CONCENTRIC: --command-radius (18) − --command-padding (4) = 14px. The search field sits flush against the panel padding, so its corner must be derived like any other nested box — it was rounded-lg (a fixed step) and stopped matching the moment the panel radius moved.

command.item.radius.description: CONCENTRIC: --command-radius − --command-padding = 10px. A nested corner must be the outer radius MINUS the padding, or the gap at the corner is not the gap along the edge — the inner box looks either pinched or rattling around inside its container. Was picked off the radius scale by eye; now it is derived.

command.item.radiusInDialog.description: Items get a larger radius when the command lives inside a dialog.

command.radius.description: rounded-xl! — also the radius of CommandDialog's content.

command.separator.bleedX.description: Bleeds past the list padding (-mx-1) so the rule spans the full panel width.

contextMenu.item.indicatorInset.description: The check sits on the RIGHT edge (absolute right-2) — unlike Dropdown menu, where it leads the row.

contextMenu.item.radius.description: CONCENTRIC: --context-menu-content-radius − --context-menu-content-padding = 6px. A nested corner must be the outer radius MINUS the padding, or the gap at the corner is not the gap along the edge — the inner box looks either pinched or rattling around inside its container. Was picked off the radius scale by eye; now it is derived.

contextMenu.subContent.borderWidth.description: Sub-content uses a border here, not the ring the root content uses.

contextMenu.subContent.chevronSize.description: The chevron on a row that opens a submenu. ml-auto — pinned right.

datePicker.triggerFontWeight.description: Overrides the button's font-medium — a date field should not read as a label.

datePicker.triggerWidth.description: w-[240px] — literal in source, no space token at 240px.

dialog.mobileInset.description: Viewport clamp: max-w-[calc(100%-2rem)] = 1rem inset per side.

drawer.content.maxWidth.description: Left/right drawers: sm:max-w-sm = 384px (they start at w-3/4).

drawer.content.peek.description: mt-24 / mb-24 — the gap kept between a top/bottom drawer and the far edge.

drawer.content.radius.description: rounded-t-xl / -b-xl / -l-xl / -r-xl depending on direction — the two inner corners only.

dropdownMenu.content.ringWidth.description: ring-1 ring-foreground/10 — a ring, not a border.

dropdownMenu.item.indicatorPaddingRight.description: Checkbox/radio items park the indicator on the right.

dropdownMenu.item.insetPaddingLeft.description: data-inset — reserves room for a leading check/radio indicator.

dropdownMenu.item.radius.description: CONCENTRIC: --dropdown-menu-content-radius − --dropdown-menu-content-padding = 6px. A nested corner must be the outer radius MINUS the padding, or the gap at the corner is not the gap along the edge — the inner box looks either pinched or rattling around inside its container. Was picked off the radius scale by eye; now it is derived.

dropdownMenu.separator.bleedX.description: Applied as a NEGATIVE margin (-mx-1) so the rule bleeds past the content padding.

dropdownMenu.subContent.minWidth.description: min-w-[96px] — literal in source, no space token at 96px.

empty.description.lineHeight.description: text-sm/relaxed — leading-relaxed, no leading token matches.

empty.maxWidth.description: max-w-sm = 384px, on both the header and the content blocks.

file-upload.dropzone.borderWidth.description: DASHED. The dashes say 'this space is waiting for something' — the same sentence the Empty state's border says, and it must not be solid, or the zone reads as a card you cannot drop into.

file-upload.dropzone.paddingY.description: The dropzone is TALL. It is a drop target before it is a button, and a thin strip is a target people miss.

file-upload.row.mediaSize.description: The file-type tile. It carries the STATE colour — success green, error red — because the row's own background stays neutral: a whole row of red for one failed upload buries the three that worked.

file-upload.row.progressGap.description: Bar → percentage. The number is not decoration: a bar with no number cannot tell you whether a stalled upload is at 5% or 95%.

focus.invalid.paint.description: The invalid ring's paint: color.destructive at 20% (aria-invalid:ring-destructive/20). Invalid is destructive — it is the shadcn 'error emphasis' role, not a status/* colour.

focus.invalid.shadow.description: The invalid ring — same geometry as focus.shadow (3px spread + 4px blur), destructive instead of ring. DESIGN DECISION 2026-07-13: invalid used to be a hard-edged 3px ring while focus was blurred; one system, one ring shape. NOTE: the old dark-mode bump to 40% was dropped — one value in both modes.

focus.ring.blur.description: Softens the ring's outer edge. Tailwind's ring-* utility cannot blur, so on 2026-07-13 every focus ring in components/ui was swapped from focus-visible:ring-3 + ring-ring/50 to focus-visible:shadow-(--focus-shadow) — one token, one look, and it now matches the Figma 'Focus ring' effect exactly.

focus.ring.paint.description: The ring's actual paint: color.ring at 50% (ring-ring/50). Kept as a live color-mix so it follows --ring through a brand swap — a variable alias cannot carry the 50% alpha, which is why this token exists instead of pointing straight at {color.ring}.

focus.shadow.description: The Figma 'Focus ring' effect style, expressed as CSS: 3px spread + 4px blur, brand primary at 50%. Follows --ring — it is no longer the kit's frozen grey rgb(161 161 161 / 0.5).

font.family.mono.description: Runtime stack lives in app/globals.css (--font-sans/--font-mono via next/font).

font.family.sans.description: Runtime stack lives in app/globals.css (--font-sans/--font-mono via next/font).

font.tracking.normal.description: Figma letter-spacing in px; Tailwind's em-based tracking utilities remain authoritative at runtime.

font.tracking.tight.description: Figma letter-spacing in px; Tailwind's em-based tracking utilities remain authoritative at runtime.

font.tracking.tighter.description: Figma letter-spacing in px; Tailwind's em-based tracking utilities remain authoritative at runtime.

font.tracking.wide.description: Figma letter-spacing in px; Tailwind's em-based tracking utilities remain authoritative at runtime.

font.tracking.wider.description: Figma letter-spacing in px; Tailwind's em-based tracking utilities remain authoritative at runtime.

font.tracking.widest.description: Figma letter-spacing in px; Tailwind's em-based tracking utilities remain authoritative at runtime.

icon.size.md.description: DEFAULT — the ubiquitous size-4 chrome baseline.

icon.strokeWidth.base.description: lucide default (stroke-width in a 24 viewBox). Source of truth for icons/custom/ geometry.

icon.strokeWidth.lg.description: 20px icon → 1px. Was 2px; that override could not survive a glyph swap, so it was dropped in favour of a weight the system can actually enforce.

icon.strokeWidth.md.description: 16px icon → 1px. This is the value BOUND on every glyph master in Figma.

input.clearButton.inset.description: Distance from the right edge; the field also reserves this much trailing padding so text never runs under the button.

input.clearButton.reservedPadding.description: Trailing padding while the clear button is present (icon 16 + insets).

input.clearButton.size.description: Clear (X) button — shown only when the field holds a value and `clearable` is set.

input.fontSize.md.description: Responsive in source: text-base on mobile (iOS zoom guard), md:text-sm on desktop.

inputGroup.addon.blockPaddingX.description: Block-start/-end addons run the full width.

inputGroup.addon.inlineControlPadding.description: The control tightens toward an inline addon (pl-1.5 / pr-1.5).

inputGroup.button.radius.description: rounded-[calc(var(--radius)-3px)] = 7px at the kit default — a nested-radius offset, not a scale step.

inputGroup.height.description: Collapses to h-auto when the group holds a textarea or a block-aligned addon.

inputGroup.kbd.radius.description: rounded-[calc(var(--radius)-5px)] = 5px at the kit default — nested-radius offset.

interaction.disabled-opacity.description: disabled:opacity-50 — used by every interactive component.

interaction.hover-opacity.description: Fill strength for hover on NON-brand surfaces only (secondary, destructive tints). It is NOT used on primary any more: fading a mid-tone fill toward the page lightens it, and white text on the faded teal falls to 3.38:1. Primary hover uses --primary-hover (a darker shade) instead.

interaction.secondary-hover-mix.description: Secondary hover mixes 5% foreground into the fill (color-mix in button.tsx).

item.gap.description: Between media, content and actions. Wide (16px) on purpose: the three regions are different KINDS of thing, and a tight gap makes them read as one run-on line.

kbd.gap.description: Also the gap between keys in a KbdGroup.

kbd.group.gap.description: Between caps in a KbdGroup. Each key gets its OWN cap — ⌘ and K are two caps, not one cap reading "⌘K". A keyboard has one physical key per cap, and the chip is a picture of the key.

label.lineHeight.description: leading-none — unitless, no leading token matches.

label.optionalFontSize.description: The optional marker reads smaller and lighter than the label itself.

label.requirementGap.description: Gap between the label text and its required/optional marker — 4px, HALF of label.gap (8px). The marker is part of the label, not a separate item beside it; label.gap still spaces real siblings (a slotted icon).

markdown.inlineCode.fontSize.description: text-[0.85em] — relative to the surrounding text, so it tracks whatever size the prose is set at.

markdown.inlineCode.radius.description: Bare `rounded` — Tailwind's 0.25rem default, NOT a step of the radius.ui scale.

menubar.bar.padding.description: 4px. FIX 2026-07-14: upstream ships p-[3px] — a literal that lands on no step of the space scale (2 · 4 · 6 · 8). Same fix as the Tabs list: both the code and this token moved to 4px so the value is expressible as a token at all.

menubar.item.insetPaddingLeft.description: Checkbox/radio items sit at pl-7 with the indicator absolutely placed at left-1.5.

menubar.item.radius.description: CONCENTRIC: --menubar-content-radius (10) − --menubar-content-padding (4) = 6px. A nested corner must be the outer radius MINUS the padding, or the gap at the corner stops matching the gap along the edge and the inner box looks pinched. Derived, not picked off the scale.

menubar.label.fontSize.description: text-sm here — dropdown-menu/context-menu labels are text-xs.

menubar.trigger.paddingY.description: 2px. FIX 2026-07-14: was py-[2px] — an arbitrary value for a number that was ALREADY on the scale (space.0-5). Nothing changes visually; it just stops being a literal.

nativeSelect.radiusSm.description: The small size CAPS its corner at 10px: rounded-[min(var(--radius-md),10px)]. A brand that sets a big --radius would otherwise turn a 28px-tall select into a pill. The token said 0.5rem, which was simply wrong — it tracked neither the scale nor the clamp.

navigation.indicator.height.description: Only 6px of the diamond is ever visible (h-1.5, overflow-hidden) — the rest is clipped. That is what makes it read as an arrow pointing at the open trigger rather than as a floating lozenge.

navigation.indicator.size.description: An 8×8 square rotated 45° — a diamond whose top half becomes the arrow. It is not a triangle shape; it is a square that only shows its corner.

navigation.link.radius.description: CONCENTRIC: --navigation-content-radius (10) − --navigation-content-padding (8) = 2px. A nested corner must be the outer radius MINUS the padding, or the gap at the corner stops matching the gap along the edge and the inner box looks pinched. Derived, not picked off the scale.

navigation.trigger.chevronSize.description: size-3 (12px) — SMALLER than the 16px icon default. It is punctuation, not content: it says a panel opens here, and it rotates 180° when it does.

navigation.viewport.marginTop.description: Gap between the bar and the panel that drops from it (mt-1.5). Small on purpose: the panel belongs to the trigger above it, and a bigger gap would cut it loose.

pagination.prevNextInlinePadding.description: Previous/Next tighten the icon-side padding (pl-1.5! / pr-1.5!) against the button default.

popover.close.size.description: The close button is an icon-button at size xs (24px) — small enough not to compete with the content, big enough to hit.

popover.gap.description: Between the popover's stacked blocks (header · content · footer).

radius.2xl.description: Static Figma-kit step — NOT what components render. Kept for parity with the kit export; use radius.ui.* for component work.

radius.3xl.description: Static Figma-kit step — NOT what components render. Kept for parity with the kit export; use radius.ui.* for component work.

radius.4xl.description: Static Figma-kit step — NOT what components render. Kept for parity with the kit export; use radius.ui.* for component work.

radius.full.description: Static Figma-kit step — NOT what components render. Kept for parity with the kit export; use radius.ui.* for component work.

radius.lg.description: Static Figma-kit step — NOT what components render. Kept for parity with the kit export; use radius.ui.* for component work.

radius.md.description: Static Figma-kit step — NOT what components render. Kept for parity with the kit export; use radius.ui.* for component work.

radius.none.description: Static Figma-kit step — NOT what components render. Kept for parity with the kit export; use radius.ui.* for component work.

radius.sm.description: Static Figma-kit step — NOT what components render. Kept for parity with the kit export; use radius.ui.* for component work.

radius.ui.2xl.description: rounded-2xl = 24px at the current --radius (0.75rem). Offsets, not ratios: a ratio lands off the 4px grid (0.75 × 12 = 9).

radius.ui.3xl.description: rounded-3xl = 32px at the current --radius (0.75rem). Offsets, not ratios: a ratio lands off the 4px grid (0.75 × 12 = 9).

radius.ui.4xl.description: rounded-4xl = 40px at the current --radius (0.75rem). Offsets, not ratios: a ratio lands off the 4px grid (0.75 × 12 = 9).

radius.ui.lg.description: rounded-lg = 12px at the current --radius (0.75rem). Offsets, not ratios: a ratio lands off the 4px grid (0.75 × 12 = 9).

radius.ui.md.description: rounded-md = 8px at the current --radius (0.75rem). Offsets, not ratios: a ratio lands off the 4px grid (0.75 × 12 = 9).

radius.ui.sm.description: rounded-sm = 4px at the current --radius (0.75rem). Offsets, not ratios: a ratio lands off the 4px grid (0.75 × 12 = 9).

radius.ui.xl.description: rounded-xl = 16px at the current --radius (0.75rem). Offsets, not ratios: a ratio lands off the 4px grid (0.75 × 12 = 9).

radius.xl.description: Static Figma-kit step — NOT what components render. Kept for parity with the kit export; use radius.ui.* for component work.

radius.xs.description: Static Figma-kit step — NOT what components render. Kept for parity with the kit export; use radius.ui.* for component work.

resizable.handle.hitArea.description: The invisible grab zone. A 1px line you must hit exactly is a line nobody can drag.

resizable.handle.thickness.description: The handle IS a 1px rule — the hit area around it is much bigger than the line you can see, which is why it works at all.

scroll-area.scrollbar.size.description: 8px. Thin enough to stay out of the way, thick enough to grab — the OS scrollbar it replaces is the thing being fixed, not imitated.

select.content.maxHeight.description: 5 rows + the content padding = 148px at the current values. Kept as a live calc so changing maxVisibleItems or the row height moves it. The rendered max-height is min(this, --radix-select-content-available-height) so the menu never overflows the viewport either.

select.content.maxVisibleItems.description: DESIGN DECISION 2026-07-13: the menu shows 5 rows and scrolls past that. Five is enough to scan without the popover swallowing the page; beyond it, a scrollbar is a clearer affordance than a list that runs off-screen.

select.content.radius.description: Corrected in Phase 4: source uses rounded-lg (the Phase-1 draft said rounded-md).

select.item.gap.description: Between the leading icon and the label.

select.item.height.description: One row: py-1 (4+4) + the 20px line box of text-sm = 28px. This is measured, not guessed — the max-height below is built from it.

select.item.radius.description: CONCENTRIC: --select-content-radius (10) − --select-content-padding (4) = 6px. A nested corner must be the outer radius MINUS the padding, or the gap at the corner stops matching the gap along the edge and the inner box looks pinched. Derived, not picked off the scale.

shadow.focus.description: Mirrors the kit's Focus ring effect.

sidebar.defaultWidth.description: 256px expanded. Wide enough for a label plus an icon plus a badge without truncating — the moment labels truncate, a sidebar stops being scannable and becomes a guessing game.

sidebar.iconWidth.description: 48px collapsed. The rail keeps ONLY the icons; nothing shrinks — the labels are removed. A sidebar that scales its text down to fit is unreadable at both sizes.

sidebar.menuBadge.inset.description: Pinned to the right edge of the row.

sidebar.menuBadge.minWidth.description: A square floor so a single digit does not render as a sliver.

sidebar.menuButton.height.description: A menu row: 32px at the default size.

sidebar.menuButton.heightLg.description: The lg row (48px) — used by the header entry that carries an avatar or a workspace switcher.

sidebar.menuSub.borderWidth.description: A 1px rail down the left. It is the only thing tying the children to the parent once you have scrolled the parent off screen.

sidebar.menuSub.gap.description: Sub-rows sit tighter than top-level rows: they are a detail of the parent, not peers of it.

sidebar.menuSub.marginX.description: The submenu is indented 14px — enough that the rail line lands under the parent's ICON, so the tree reads as 'these belong to that'.

sidebar.menuSubButton.height.description: 28px — shorter than the 32px parent row. The size difference IS the hierarchy.

sidebar.padding.description: Padding of header, footer and each group.

size.14.description: Icon sm — breadcrumb chevron, copy buttons.

size.16.description: Icon md — the default icon size across the library.

size.20.description: Icon lg. Also kbd / token (size sm) height.

size.8.description: Smallest box in the system — avatar badge (size sm).

size.control.lg.description: h-9 — button size lg, combobox trigger min-height.

size.control.md.description: h-8 — button/input DEFAULT, select trigger, tabs list, avatar default.

size.control.sm.description: h-7 — button size sm, input size sm, toggle sm.

size.control.xl.description: 44px — the mobile touch-target floor (iOS HIG 44pt / Material 48dp). DESIGN DECISION 2026-07-13: xl is the size you reach for on touch, so it is no longer the measured h-10 (40px) — it is 44. Consumers that are var-driven (button, input, select trigger, icon-button) pick this up with no code change. Boxes that merely BORROWED this step and are not touch targets (avatar lg, alert-dialog media, table header row) were re-pointed to {size.40} so they did not silently grow.

size.control.xs.description: h-6 — button size xs / icon-xs, avatar sm.

spinner.duration.description: Tailwind's animate-spin (1s linear infinite). NOT motion.duration.base (200ms) — a loop, not a transition.

spinner.size.description: size-4 — callers override for larger spinners.

status.destructive.description: Alias of the live semantic token.

status.success.description: Design sign-off 2026-07-08: green-700 (green-600 on white was 3.30:1 < AA). Not consumed yet.

status.warning.description: The warning FILL. Consumed by badge (as a 10% tint).

status.warning-foreground.description: Text ON a solid warning fill.

status.warning-text.description: Warning as TEXT (on a light/tinted surface). amber.500 is the fill colour and is far too light to read — 2.1:1 on white; amber.700 is 4.6:1. success and info need no such split: green.700 and blue.600 already pass as text.

surface.overlay.description: Modal scrim (alert-dialog/drawer/sheet): black @ 10% paired with supports-backdrop-filter:backdrop-blur-xs. DESIGN DECISION 2026-07-08: keep this (the rendered behavior); the Figma kit's 30% `background-color` extra (color.background-color) is superseded for scrim use — it remains only as a legacy kit value. Dialog itself renders no scrim. CONSUMED at runtime: bg-(--surface-overlay) in drawer.tsx, alert-dialog.tsx, sheet.tsx.

switch.track.height.md.description: Thumb 16 + a 1px gap each side. FIX 2026-07-13: was h-[18.4px] — an arbitrary value with no derivation behind it. The thumb is 16 and the sm track keeps a 1px gap on each side (12 + 1 + 1 = 14), so md is 16 + 1 + 1 = 18. A fractional px cannot land on the size scale, renders soft on non-retina, and makes the two sizes follow different rules for no reason.

table.cellMinHeight.description: Every cell is at least 48px tall, whatever it holds. Without this the row height follows its tallest cell — a text cell is 32, a cell with an input is 44, one with a two-line value is 52 — and the rows come out ragged, so the eye has to re-find every column on the way down. 48 also clears the touch floor for the controls that live in cells.

table.headerFontWeight.description: The header is the only row in the table that is not data — weight is what says so.

table.headerHeight.description: Table header is a row, not a touch target — pinned to 40px when size.control.xl moved to 44.

table.rowBorderWidth.description: Every row carries its own bottom border — the LAST one drops it, so the table does not end in a line that looks like a broken row.

table.sortIconSize.description: The sort affordance. Present only on columns that CAN be sorted — a sort icon on every column teaches people it means nothing.

tabs.list.gap.description: ZERO. The triggers sit flush; the active tab is separated by its FILL, not by a gap. A gap here would make the inactive tabs read as separate buttons.

tabs.list.padding.description: 4px. FIX 2026-07-14: upstream shadcn ships p-[3px] — an arbitrary literal that lands on no step of the space scale (which goes 2 · 4 · 6 · 8). 3px cannot be expressed as a token without inventing one, so both the code and this token moved to 4px. The active tab renders 2px shorter than before; nothing else changes.

tabs.trigger.radius.description: CONCENTRIC: --tabs-list-radius − --tabs-list-padding = 6px. A nested corner must be the outer radius MINUS the padding, or the gap at the corner is not the gap along the edge — the inner box looks either pinched or rattling around inside its container. Was picked off the radius scale by eye; now it is derived.

textarea.clearButton.reservedPadding.description: Trailing padding while the clear button is present.

textarea.clearButton.size.description: Clear (X) button — an Icon instance, glyph = close. NOT in textarea.tsx yet; the design calls for it and the code should follow (see Input).

textarea.fontSize.md.description: text-base on mobile, text-sm from md up — the desktop value is canonical.

textarea.resizeGrip.inset.description: How far the grip sits from the corner.

textarea.resizeGrip.size.description: The native resize grip the browser paints in the bottom-right corner. textarea has NO resize-none, so it IS resizable — this is real, not decoration. (The textarea inside input-group DOES set resize-none and shows no grip.)

theme.dashboard.defaultDensity.description: Pairs with tokens/mode/compact.json once density modes are wired.

theme.healthcare.defaultDensity.description: Pairs with tokens/mode/comfortable.json once density modes are wired.

theme.looloo.color.accent.description: Signed off: #0B2846 (deep navy; no matching ramp step — literal).

theme.looloo.color.primary-foreground.description: Dark text on the teal primary — 10.64:1 (white would be 1.86:1).

theme.neutral.radius.description: Base --radius; runtime scale is calc-based in app/globals.css.

theme.virtual-agent.radius.description: Base --radius; runtime scale is calc-based in app/globals.css.

toggle.fontSize.sm.description: 12px. FIX 2026-07-14: was text-[0.8rem] (12.8px) — an arbitrary value that exists on no type scale and renders on a half-pixel. 12 is the xs step we already have.

toggle.paddingX.description: Same at every size; tightened to space.2 / space.1-5 when an inline icon is present.

toggleGroup.gap.description: Default `spacing` prop = 2 → gap-[--spacing(2)] = 8px. spacing={0} joins the items.

toggleGroup.joinedPaddingX.description: spacing=0 items tighten from px-2.5 to px-2.

toggleGroup.radiusSm.description: Size sm clamps: rounded-[min(var(--radius-md),10px)] → 8px at the kit default.

token.remove.inset.description: ml-0.5 with a matching -mr-0.5 so the button hugs the pill's right edge.

toolbar.button.focusRingWidth.description: focus-visible:ring-2 — 2px, not the 3px focus.ring.width the form controls use.

toolbar.button.radius.description: CONCENTRIC: --toolbar-radius − --toolbar-padding = 4px. A nested corner must be the outer radius MINUS the padding, or the gap at the corner is not the gap along the edge — the inner box looks either pinched or rattling around inside its container. Was picked off the radius scale by eye; now it is derived.
